growth_records.medium: Brackish carbonate-buffered sulfate-reducer medium (Widdel & Bak 1992, without sulfate) + North Sea crude oil
growth_records.cite: Gray et al. The quantitative significance of Syntrophaceae and syntrophic partnerships in methanogenic degradation of crude oil alkanes. Environmental Microbiology (2011). DOI:10.1111/j.1462-2920.2011.02570.x. [PMC3258425]
growth_records.snippet: ...0.01 day-1... Methanosaetaceae required 69 days... growth yields... 3.0-3.5 x 10-6... 4.6-5.5 x 10-5 g(cell-C)/mol(CH4)
growth_records.notes: Environmental microcosm, mixed community. mu = 0.01 day-1 (qPCR fit R2=0.73) = 0.01/24 = 4.2e-4 /h; doubling time reported 69 days = 1656 h. Very low growth yields reported 3.0-3.5e-6 g cell-C/g alkane-C and 4.6-5.5e-5 g cell-C/mol CH4, consistent with minor contribution. Temperature/pH not stated.
